1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"io"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		workguard "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
13		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
14	)
15	
16	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
17	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
18	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
19	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
20	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
21	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		cmd := &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "work",
24			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
25			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
26	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
27	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
28	
29	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
30	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
31	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
32	bounds.
33	
34	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
35	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
36	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
37	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
38	
39	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
40	  drained     — no ready beads remain
41	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
42	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
43	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
44	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
45	
46	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
47	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
48	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
49	`,
50			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
51	  ddx work
52	
53	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
54	  ddx work --once
55	
56	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
57	  ddx work --watch
58	
59	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
60	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
61	
62	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
63	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
64	
65	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
66	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
67	
68	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
69	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
70			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
71			RunE: f.runWork,
72		}
73	
74		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
75		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
76		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
77		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
78		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
79		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
80		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
81		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
82		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
84		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
85		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
87		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
88		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
89		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
90		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
91		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
92		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
93		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
94		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
95		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-recovery-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxRecoveryCostUSD, "Per-bead automated recovery budget in USD for reframe/decompose attempts after repeated ladder exhaustion")
96		cmd.Flags().Duration("preclaim-timeout", workguard.DefaultPreClaimTimeout, "Pre-claim readiness timeout for preflight/readiness hooks")
97		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
98		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
99		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
100			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
101		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
102		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
103		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
104		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
105	
106		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
107		// "ddx work metrics", "ddx work analyze", and "ddx work status" as
108		// subcommands.
109		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
110		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
111		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
112		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkMetricsCommand())
113		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkAnalyzeCommand())
114		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkStatusCommand())
115	
116		return cmd
117	}
118	
119	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
120	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
121	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
122	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
123	// by this layer.
124	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
125		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128	
129		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
130		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
131	
132		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
133		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
134		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
135		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
136		if !asJSON {
137			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
138			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
139				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
140			})
141		}
142	
143		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
144	}
145	
146	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
147		if asJSON {
148			payload := struct {
149				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
150				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
151			}{
152				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
153				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
154			}
155			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
156			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
157			return enc.Encode(payload)
158		}
159	
160		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
161			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
162			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
163			return nil
164		}
165	
166		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
167		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
168		if result.Failures > 0 {
169			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
170			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
171				if attempt.Status != "success" {
172					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
173					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
174						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
175					}
176					fmt.Fprintln(w)
177				}
178			}
179		}
180		if result.NoReadyWork {
181			fmt.Fprintln(w)
182			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
183			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
184		}
185		return nil
186	}
187	
188	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
189		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
190		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
191		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
192			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
193	}
194	
195	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
196		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
197			switch attempt.Status {
198			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
199				closed++
200				changed++
201			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
202				closed++
203				alreadySatisfied++
204			}
205		}
206		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
207			closed = result.Successes
208		}
209		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
210	}
211	
212	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
213		switch result.ExitReason {
214		case "drained":
215			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
216		case "once_complete":
217			return "once complete"
218		case "budget":
219			return "budget reached"
220		case "no_progress":
221			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
222		case "blocked":
223			return "blocked waiting for external action"
224		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
225			return "stopped by signal"
226		case "fatal_config":
227			return "fatal configuration error"
228		case "preflight_failed":
229			return "preflight failed"
230		case "resource_exhausted":
231			return "resource exhausted"
232		case "routing_unavailable":
233			return "routing unavailable"
234		}
235		if result.StopCondition != "" {
236			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
237		}
238		return "completed"
239	}
240	
241	func writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, snapshot *agent.QueueSnapshot) {
242		if snapshot == nil {
243			return
244		}
245		retry := ""
246		if snapshot.NextRetryAfter != "" {
247			retry = " next-retry=" + snapshot.NextRetryAfter
248		}
249		fmt.Fprintf(w, "remaining queue: execution-ready=%d blocked=%d operator-attention=%d needs-human/investigation=%d cooldown/deferred=%d%s execution-ineligible=%d superseded=%d epics=%d epic-closure-candidates=%d\n",
250			snapshot.ExecutionReadyCount,
251			snapshot.BlockedCount,
252			snapshot.ProposedOperatorAttentionCount,
253			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount,
254			snapshot.RetryCooldownCount,
255			retry,
256			snapshot.ExecutionIneligibleCount,
257			snapshot.SupersededCount,
258			snapshot.SkippedEpicsCount,
259			snapshot.EpicClosureCandidatesCount,
260		)
261		if snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal <= 0 || snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount <= 0 {
262			return
263		}
264		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d beads blocked behind %d needs-human blockers:\n",
265			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal, snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
266		for i, blocker := range snapshot.HumanReviewBlockers {
267			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %d. %s %s (%d downstream)\n", i+1, blocker.ID, blocker.Title, blocker.DownstreamBlockedCount)
268		}
269	}
270	
271	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
272		if projectRoot != "" {
273			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
274		}
275		fmt.Fprintln(w, "No execution-ready beads.")
276		if noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d) {
277			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  queue drained: no open work remains in lifecycle queues.")
278		}
279		if len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) > 0 {
280			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  operator attention: %d proposed bead(s) stop autonomous work and may block downstream dependents: %s\n",
281				len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention), strings.Join(d.ProposedOperatorAttention, ", "))
282		}
283		if len(d.DependencyWaiting) > 0 {
284			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  waiting on dependencies: %d open bead(s): %s\n",
285				len(d.DependencyWaiting), strings.Join(d.DependencyWaiting, ", "))
286		}
287		if len(d.ExternalBlocked) > 0 {
288			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  external blocked: %d bead(s) with explicit blocked status: %s\n",
289				len(d.ExternalBlocked), strings.Join(d.ExternalBlocked, ", "))
290		}
291		if len(d.RetryCooldown) > 0 {
292			retryHint := ""
293			if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
294				retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
295			}
296			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  retry cooldown: %d bead(s)%s: %s\n",
297				len(d.RetryCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.RetryCooldown, ", "))
298		}
299		if len(d.NotEligible) > 0 {
300			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  not execution eligible: %d bead(s): %s\n",
301				len(d.NotEligible), strings.Join(d.NotEligible, ", "))
302		}
303		if len(d.Superseded) > 0 {
304			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  superseded: %d bead(s): %s\n",
305				len(d.Superseded), strings.Join(d.Superseded, ", "))
306		}
307		if len(d.Epics) > 0 {
308			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  epic containers: %d ready epic(s) with open children (decompose into tasks): %s\n",
309				len(d.Epics), strings.Join(d.Epics, ", "))
310		}
311		if len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
312			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
313				strings.Join(d.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
314		}
315	}
316	
317	func noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) bool {
318		return len(d.ExecutionReady) == 0 &&
319			len(d.DependencyWaiting) == 0 &&
320			len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) == 0 &&
321			len(d.RetryCooldown) == 0 &&
322			len(d.ExternalBlocked) == 0 &&
323			len(d.NotEligible) == 0 &&
324			len(d.Superseded) == 0 &&
325			len(d.Epics) == 0 &&
326			len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) == 0 &&
327			d.NextRetryAfter == ""
328	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"strings"
6		"testing"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)
13	
14	// TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly proves the default scope of
15	// `ddx work status --json` is the requested project root, not every live
16	// ddx worker on the host. This is the regression test for the originating
17	// incident: a global `ps | grep ddx work` scan answered "is project A's
18	// worker running?" with a worker that actually belonged to project B.
19	func TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly(t *testing.T) {
20		projectA := t.TempDir()
21		projectB := t.TempDir()
22	
23		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
24			{
25				PID:         101,
26				Command:     "ddx work --once --project " + projectA,
27				ProjectRoot: projectA,
28				StartedAt:   time.Now().Add(-10 * time.Minute).UTC(),
29				Age:         "10m",
30				AgeSeconds:  600,
31			},
32			{
33				PID:         202,
34				Command:     "ddx work --once --project " + projectB,
35				ProjectRoot: projectB,
36				StartedAt:   time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Minute).UTC(),
37				Age:         "3m",
38				AgeSeconds:  180,
39			},
40		}
41	
42		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectA)
43		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
44		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
45	
46		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA, "--json")
47		require.NoError(t, err)
48	
49		var report WorkStatusReport
50		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
51	
52		assert.Equal(t, "project", report.Scope)
53		assert.Equal(t, projectA, report.ProjectRoot)
54		require.Len(t, report.Workers, 1, "default scope must filter to requested project only; got %s", out)
55		assert.Equal(t, 101, report.Workers[0].PID)
56		assert.Equal(t, projectA, report.Workers[0].ProjectRoot)
57	}
58	
59	// TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView proves --all-projects
60	// returns every live worker (labelled with their own project_root) so an
61	// operator can explicitly opt into the cross-project view.
62	func TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView(t *testing.T) {
63		projectA := t.TempDir()
64		projectB := t.TempDir()
65	
66		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
67			{PID: 11, Command: "ddx work", ProjectRoot: projectA, StartedAt: time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Minute)},
68			{PID: 22, Command: "ddx try ddx-abc12345", ProjectRoot: projectB, StartedAt: time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Minute)},
69		}
70	
71		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectA)
72		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
73		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
74	
75		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA, "--all-projects", "--json")
76		require.NoError(t, err)
77	
78		var report WorkStatusReport
79		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
80	
81		assert.Equal(t, "all-projects", report.Scope)
82		require.Len(t, report.Workers, 2)
83		projects := make([]string, 0, 2)
84		for _, w := range report.Workers {
85			projects = append(projects, w.ProjectRoot)
86			assert.NotEmpty(t, w.ProjectRoot, "every worker entry must carry its own project_root")
87		}
88		assert.Contains(t, projects, projectA)
89		assert.Contains(t, projects, projectB)
90	}
91	
92	// TestWorkStatusNoLiveWorkersNamesProjectRoot proves the empty state
93	// names the resolved project root rather than falling back to silence or
94	// to unrelated processes. This is the exact failure surface the bead
95	// originated from: an empty answer must not be answered with another
96	// project's process.
97	func TestWorkStatusNoLiveWorkersNamesProjectRoot(t *testing.T) {
98		projectA := t.TempDir()
99		projectB := t.TempDir()
100	
101		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
102			{PID: 999, Command: "ddx work --project " + projectB, ProjectRoot: projectB, StartedAt: time.Now()},
103		}
104	
105		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectA)
106		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
107		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
108	
109		// Text output
110		textOut, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA)
111		require.NoError(t, err)
112		assert.Contains(t, textOut, "No live ddx workers for project")
113		assert.Contains(t, textOut, projectA,
114			"empty state must name the resolved project root, not fall back to unrelated workers")
115		assert.NotContains(t, textOut, projectB,
116			"empty state must not surface another project's worker as evidence")
117	
118		// JSON output for the same condition
119		jsonOut, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA, "--json")
120		require.NoError(t, err)
121		var report WorkStatusReport
122		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonOut), &report))
123		assert.Equal(t, projectA, report.ProjectRoot)
124		assert.Empty(t, report.Workers)
125	}
126	
127	// TestWorkStatusInfersBeadAndExecutionWorktree proves a live inline
128	// `ddx work` / `ddx try` process exposes pid, age, command, project_root,
129	// and the active bead ID when the command line or execution worktree
130	// contains one.
131	func TestWorkStatusInfersBeadAndExecutionWorktree(t *testing.T) {
132		projectA := t.TempDir()
133	
134		worktree := "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c3219628-20260513T231309-0e6f776b"
135		cmdline := "ddx work --once --project " + projectA
136		beadID, inferredWorktree := workerstatus.InferBead(cmdline, worktree)
137		require.Equal(t, "ddx-c3219628", beadID,
138			"InferBead must extract the bead id from an execute-bead worktree path")
139		require.Equal(t, worktree, inferredWorktree)
140	
141		scannerWorkers := []workerstatus.LiveWorker{
142			{
143				PID:               12345,
144				Command:           cmdline,
145				ProjectRoot:       projectA,
146				StartedAt:         time.Now().Add(-90 * time.Second).UTC(),
147				Age:               "1m",
148				AgeSeconds:        90,
149				BeadID:            beadID,
150				ExecutionWorktree: inferredWorktree,
151				Cwd:               worktree,
152			},
153		}
154	
155		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectA)
156		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: scannerWorkers}
157		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
158	
159		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--project", projectA, "--json")
160		require.NoError(t, err)
161	
162		var report WorkStatusReport
163		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
164		require.Len(t, report.Workers, 1)
165		w := report.Workers[0]
166		assert.Equal(t, 12345, w.PID)
167		assert.NotZero(t, w.AgeSeconds, "age must be exposed")
168		assert.NotEmpty(t, w.Age, "human-readable age must be set")
169		assert.Equal(t, cmdline, w.Command)
170		assert.Equal(t, projectA, w.ProjectRoot)
171		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-c3219628", w.BeadID)
172		assert.Equal(t, worktree, w.ExecutionWorktree)
173	}
174	
175	// TestWorkStatus_EmptyAllProjectsMessage verifies the all-projects empty
176	// state speaks about the host rather than naming a project root (which
177	// would be misleading when the user opted into the cross-project view).
178	func TestWorkStatus_EmptyAllProjectsMessage(t *testing.T) {
179		factory := NewCommandFactory(t.TempDir())
180		factory.workerScannerOverride = fixedScanner{workers: nil}
181		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
182	
183		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "status", "--all-projects")
184		require.NoError(t, err)
185		assert.True(t, strings.Contains(out, "No live ddx workers found on this host."),
186			"expected host-scoped empty message, got %q", out)
187	}
188	
189	// TestWorkStatusFlagsArePresent guards the public flag surface.
190	func TestWorkStatusFlagsArePresent(t *testing.T) {
191		dir := t.TempDir()
192		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
193	
194		statusCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work", "status"})
195		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work status must exist")
196		require.NotNil(t, statusCmd.Flags().Lookup("project"))
197		require.NotNil(t, statusCmd.Flags().Lookup("all-projects"))
198		require.NotNil(t, statusCmd.Flags().Lookup("json"))
199	}

go: downloading github.com/easel/fizeau v0.12.3
ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd	0.025s

1	# Status — Queue State and Health
2	
3	## Mode note
4	
5	This reference covers **direct queue queries and health checks** (`queue_steward`
6	and `interactive-steward` orient phase). Use it for:
7	
8	- "What's on the queue?" "What's ready?" "Is DDx healthy?" "Sync status?"
9	
10	If the user is asking a **broad planning or orientation question** — "what should
11	I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?" — see `reference/interactive.md`
12	first. The interactive steward uses these commands to build `QueueFacts` but
13	does not instruct manual ready-bead implementation; `ddx work` is the queue
14	worker.
15	
16	---
17	
18	Three distinct intents live here. They answer different questions
19	and have different commands; call them by intent, not
20	interchangeably.
21	
22	## What's on the queue?
23	
24	"What's ready to work on?" "What's blocked?"
25	
26	```bash
27	ddx bead ready      # execution-ready open beads; use --execution for worker view
28	ddx bead blocked    # status=blocked external/recheckable blockers
29	ddx bead status     # aggregate lifecycle and derived queue counts
30	ddx bead list       # all beads (filter with --status, --label, --where)
31	```
32	
33	Typical answers:
34	
35	- "What should I work on next?" → See `reference/interactive.md` for the
36	  interactive-steward planning workflow. The steward uses `ddx bead ready` to
37	  build `QueueFacts` but will not instruct you to manually implement ready beads.
38	- "Why isn't bead X moving?" → `ddx bead show <id>` for full state;
39	  `ddx bead dep tree <id>` to see what it depends on.
40	- "How many beads are open right now?" → `ddx bead status`.
41	- "What is waiting on dependencies?" → `ddx bead ready` / `ddx bead status`;
42	  dependency waits are derived queue state, not `status=blocked`.
43	
44	## Are workers running for this project?
45	
46	"Is the worker still working beads for this project?" "How is the queue
47	progressing?"
48	
49	```bash
50	ddx work status            # live ddx work/ddx try processes for THIS project
51	ddx work status --json     # machine-readable
52	ddx work status --all-projects   # explicit cross-project escape hatch
53	```
54	
55	`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
56	`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → git root of CWD). The default view never reports
57	workers from another repository — surfacing a worker that belongs to a
58	different project as evidence that this project is "still working" is the
59	exact failure this command was added to prevent.
60	
61	**Required when answering "is the worker still running for project X?":**
62	
63	1. Resolve the requested project root before answering. Pass `--project`
64	   if you are not already in that root.
65	2. Use `ddx work status` (or `ddx work status --json`) for the live-worker
66	   evidence. Never substitute a raw global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
67	   scan: it returns workers from every checkout on the host and produces
68	   a misleading "yes, working" signal for whichever project happens to be
69	   asked.
70	3. Always name the project root the worker status applies to ("for
71	   `<project>`: …"). A bare "yes, a worker is running" answer is unsafe
72	   because the reader cannot tell which project it refers to.
73	4. If a live worker belongs to a *different* project, mention it only as
74	   explicitly out-of-scope context (`--all-projects` view), not as
75	   evidence that the asked-about project is progressing.
76	
77	## Am I healthy?
78	
79	"Is DDx installed and working?" "Are the harnesses available?"
80	
81	```bash
82	ddx doctor          # environment, config, install validation
83	# Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed by the project, remain separate from DDx.
84	ddx version         # CLI version
85	```
86	
87	`ddx doctor` checks:
88	
89	- DDx binary is reachable and recent.
90	- `.ddx/config.yaml` is valid (schema + required fields).
91	- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is readable and parses.
92	- Git is available and the repo is in a usable state.
93	- Shell integration (where applicable) is set up.
94	- Plugins declared in config are actually installed.
95	
96	Fizeau diagnostics go further into the execution service: which
97	harnesses are discoverable, whether provider credentials are present,
98	and whether routing metadata loads.
99	
100	## Is the project drifted from upstream?
101	
102	"Has the DDx library changed since I last updated?" "Do I need to
103	pull new plugin content?"
104	
105	```bash
106	ddx status          # sync state: upstream revs, local drift, stale docs
107	ddx doc stale       # documents that reference outdated artifacts
108	```
109	
110	`ddx status` is the CLI-wide status: binary version, plugin
111	versions, document-graph freshness. `ddx doc stale` specifically
112	lists documents that depend on artifacts that have moved or
113	changed.
114	
115	## Don't use these interchangeably
116	
117	- `ddx doctor` — **environment health** (is DDx working on this
118	  machine?). Call after `ddx upgrade` or when things seem broken.
119	- Fizeau diagnostics — **harness health** (are the routes reachable?).
120	  Call when `ddx run` reports an availability failure.
121	- `ddx status` — **upstream drift** (is this project in sync with
122	  the library registry?). Call periodically to catch stale
123	  plugins.
124	- `ddx bead status` — **work queue state** (how many beads, in
125	  what states?). Call to understand project progress.
126	
127	A user asking "how's the project doing?" probably means `ddx
128	doctor` or the Fizeau diagnostics surface (am I set up correctly) —
129	follow up with `ddx bead status` if they want work-queue state too.
130	
131	A user asking "what's ready to work on?" is clearly asking for
132	`ddx bead ready`.
133	
134	## Anti-patterns
135	
136	- **Running `ddx status` to check if routes work.** Use
137	  the Fizeau diagnostics surface for that; `ddx status` is about upstream sync.
138	- **Running `ddx doctor` to see the queue.** Use `ddx bead status`.
139	- **Closing stale beads based on "status" alone.** `ddx bead
140	  status` shows counts, not quality. Use `ddx bead list
141	  --status open` + `ddx bead show <id>` to actually read each bead.
142	- **Answering "is the worker running?" with a global `ps aux | grep -E
143	  'ddx work|ddx try'` scan.** Without project-root filtering this surfaces
144	  workers from other repositories and falsely signals progress on the
145	  project that was asked about. Use `ddx work status` (project-scoped by
146	  default) and only fall back to `--all-projects` for an explicit
147	  cross-project view.
148	
149	## CLI reference
150	
151	```bash
152	# Queue state
153	ddx bead ready
154	ddx bead blocked
155	ddx bead status
156	ddx bead list [--status open|closed] [--label <l>]
157	ddx bead show <id>
158	
159	# Live workers for this project (defaults to current project root)
160	ddx work status
161	ddx work status --json
162	ddx work status --all-projects   # opt-in cross-project view
163	
164	# Environment health
165	ddx doctor
166	ddx version
167	
168	# Agent/harness health
169	# Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed by the project, remain separate from DDx.
170	
171	# Upstream sync
172	ddx status
173	ddx doc stale
174	```
175	
176	Full flag list: `ddx doctor --help`,
177	`ddx bead --help`.
178

data.text: The bead `ddx-c3219628` was already implemented at the base revision in commit `f4ea19edf feat(work): scope live worker reports to the requested project [ddx-c3219628]`. The CLI command `ddx work status`, the `workerstatus` package, all four required tests, and the skill documentation in all three skill trees already exist. All tests pass; `ddx skills check` validates.

26fec7ee76f04aba43cce8187d305164628327db. The implementation landed in
commit f4ea19edf "feat(work): scope live worker reports to the requested
project [ddx-c3219628]" and was preserved through the subsequent fix
4a95e919f. Concretely:

- `ddx work status` exists at cli/cmd/work_status.go with --project,
  --all-projects, and --json flags, registered as a subcommand in
  cli/cmd/work.go.
- The supporting package lives at cli/internal/workerstatus/ and
  filters live workers by canonical project root.
- AC1-AC4 tests (TestWorkStatusDefaultsToCurrentProjectOnly,
  TestWorkStatusAllProjectsShowsExplicitGlobalView,
  TestWorkStatusNoLiveWorkersNamesProjectRoot,
  TestWorkStatusInfersBeadAndExecutionWorktree) exist in
  cli/cmd/work_status_test.go and pass.
- AC5/AC6 skill docs already document the project-scoped worker-status
  rule and the --all-projects escape hatch in all three skill trees:
  .agents/skills/ddx/reference/{status,work}.md,
  .claude/skills/ddx/reference/{status,work}.md, and
  cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/{status,work}.md (verified
  byte-identical with diff).
- `ddx skills check` validates the three skill trees ("validated 24
  skill files").

No code or doc change is required. The verification_command above is
the AC7 test invocation, which exits 0 against the current tree.
